channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Bam Adebayo's 83-point night was one to remember. But not everyone was pleased
item.description: Detractors point to Adebayo's one-of-a-kind stat line — 43 field goal attempts, 22 3-point attempts and, most of all, NBA records of 36 free throws and 43 attempts — as proof of stat-padding.
item.content:encoded:
Detractors point to Adebayo's one-of-a-kind stat line — 43 field goal attempts, 22 3-point attempts and, most of all, NBA records of 36 free throws and 43 attempts — as proof of stat-padding.

(Image credit: Rebecca Blackwell)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says Democrats must cheat to win. What do his supporters think?
item.description: NPR spent several days traveling across a pair of swing districts in Pennsylvania to find out. The answers show how much has changed since the 2020 election.
item.content:encoded:
NPR spent several days traveling across a pair of swing districts in Pennsylvania to find out. The answers show how much has changed since the 2020 election.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: The government is investigating new claims that DOGE misused Social Security data
item.description: The fallout from DOGE staffers' efforts to access sensitive Social Security data continues as an agency watchdog disclosed a new investigation into "potential misuse" reported by a whistleblower.
item.content:encoded:
The fallout from DOGE staffers' efforts to access sensitive Social Security data continues as an agency watchdog disclosed a new investigation into "potential misuse" reported by a whistleblower.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Epstein's longtime accountant testifies he was 'not aware' of sex offender's crimes
item.description: Richard Kahn testified to the House Oversight Committee that he did not know about Epstein's crimes. He said monetary gifts that Epstein made did not raise any red flags.
item.content:encoded:
Richard Kahn testified to the House Oversight Committee that he did not know about Epstein's crimes. He said monetary gifts that Epstein made did not raise any red flags.

(Image credit: Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Rebecca Gayheart Dane on caring for her late husband, Eric Dane, and synthetic voices
item.description: The wife of 'Grey's Anatomy' actor Eric Dane says caring for him gave her an "extra dose" of compassion for others.
item.content:encoded:
The wife of 'Grey's Anatomy' actor Eric Dane says caring for him gave her an "extra dose" of compassion for others.

(Image credit: Alberto E. Rodriguez / Getty Images for Chrysalis Butterfly Ball)
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: Chile turns right: Kast inaugurated as nation's most conservative leader since Pinochet
item.description: Chile has sworn in its most right-wing president in decades — and his rise, and ideology, are rooted in a small town beneath the Andes.
item.content:encoded:
Chile has sworn in its most right-wing president in decades — and his rise, and ideology, are rooted in a small town beneath the Andes.

(Image credit: Gustavo Garello)
item.dc:creator: John Bartlett

item.title: Iran's soccer team cannot participate in the FIFA World Cup, Iranian minister says
item.description: Iran is set to play three games in the U.S. this June. But amid the U.S.-Israel military campaign that has killed Iran's supreme leader, Iran's sports minister said the team would pull out.
item.content:encoded:
Iran is set to play three games in the U.S. this June. But amid the U.S.-Israel military campaign that has killed Iran's supreme leader, Iran's sports minister said the team would pull out.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Pentagon probe points to U.S. missile hitting Iranian school
item.description: A military assessment suggests a U.S. Tomahawk cruise missile was responsible for at least 165 deaths at an Iranian girls' school, according to a U.S. official who was not authorized to speak publicly.
item.content:encoded:
A military assessment suggests a U.S. Tomahawk cruise missile was responsible for at least 165 deaths at an Iranian girls' school, according to a U.S. official who was not authorized to speak publicly.

(Image credit: Ali Najafii)
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: No Nobles Day: Britain's Parliament boots its last hereditary Lords after 700 years
item.description: Government minister Nick Thomas-Symonds said the change put an end to "an archaic and undemocratic principle." The removed aristocrats are 92 of the House of Lords' 800 members.
item.content:encoded:
Government minister Nick Thomas-Symonds said the change put an end to "an archaic and undemocratic principle." The removed aristocrats are 92 of the House of Lords' 800 members.

(Image credit: Kirsty Wigglesworth)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How the Iran war is disrupting air travel -- and advice if you're planning a trip
item.description: The war in Iran is roiling jet fuel prices and airlines are beginning to hike prices, unsettling travelers far from the Middle East. If you're booking a flight soon, here are things to know.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran is roiling jet fuel prices and airlines are beginning to hike prices, unsettling travelers far from the Middle East. If you're booking a flight soon, here are things to know.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell
